constraint: If MOA copy is used, use exactly one approved MOA variant verbatim.

quotes: ...LISRAYA works by turning off the specific inflammatory switches that are stuck in the 'on' position.
Do more of the activities you love
LISRAYA is a once-daily FDA-approved pill that blocks the specific inflammatory signals that drive dermatomyositis, so you can:
LISRAYA is a once-daily pill—you can take it at home or anywhere you like, with no shots or infusions required.
Talk to your doctor about LISRAYA, the DM treatment you may be looking for.
Unlike other treatments for dermatomyositis (DM), which suppress the immune system generally, LISRAYA targets the root cause of the disease.
YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.
My Compass Support (patient support: Personalized Communication via PAL — text, phone, email, in person; Authorization Support).

governance.applicability: when using the applicable campaign-message module; not a requirement to include every module

intent: Keep patient-facing campaign claims, CTA language, and support-program naming within the approved messaging framework.
rule_text: Use the approved campaign messaging verbatim: headline “YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.”; the stated product-benefit introduction and its four bullets; either approved MOA message; the administration message; the doctor-discussion CTA “Talk to your doctor about LISRAYA, the DM treatment you may be looking for.”; and the support-program name “My Compass Support” with its described patient-support services.
